fresh_facts.claim: US-Iran peace talks in Islamabad collapsed after 21 hours on April 12, 2026, with no deal reached.
fresh_facts.source: US official statements and multiple news reports

fresh_facts.claim: US Vice President JD Vance confirmed the talks failed and stated Iran refused US demands to commit against developing nuclear weapons.
President Donald Trump claimed US forces have neutralized Iran's naval and air capabilities.
US minesweepers and warships are operating or passing through the Strait of Hormuz.
Trump hinted at a naval blockade to choke Iranian oil exports, described as a potential Trump Card strategy.
A fragile ceasefire between the U.S., Israel, and Iran masks unresolved nuclear ambitions and destabilized Lebanon.
Iran assembled an unusually broad diplomatic team for US talks in Islamabad spanning the political spectrum.
President Trump set a deadline for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, described as final.
Masoud Pezeshkian was elected Iranian president as a nominally reformist but wields little influence due to institutional constraints.
The Guardian Council has systematically barred moderate and reformist-leaning candidates from parliamentary and presidential elections since 2020.
Inflation and poverty have worsened in Iran, and severe energy shortages in winter 2024/25 deepened economic hardship.
The Bashar Assad regime collapsed in December 2024, substantially undermining Iran's strategic influence in the Middle East.
Israel conducted the first direct attack on Iranian territory by another state since the end of the Iran-Iraq War in 1988.
Hezbollah has been weakened, calling Iran's defense doctrine into question.
The 2022 nationwide uprising in Iran gave way to increased repression and intensified crackdown on civil liberties.
Sporadic protests have periodically flared up across Iran, reflecting widespread public dissatisfaction with political and economic status quo.
Iran's regime maintains a firm grip on power and retains formidable ability to suppress opposition.
Iran's foreign policy remains largely unchanged despite tactical diplomatic efforts, further deepening Iran's economic isolation.
US experts anticipate increased US pressure on Iran's nuclear program ahead of the ceasefire's April 22 end.
Iranian officials accused the US of excessive demands lacking mutual trust in the failed negotiations.

regime_fall.current_status: U.S.-Iran peace talks in Islamabad collapsed after 21 hours on April 12, 2026 with no agreement reached, as Iran refused U.S. demands on nuclear weapons commitments. Trump administration is preparing renewed military pressure including Strait of Hormuz operations and potential naval blockade, while Iran faces severe internal economic hardship and weakened regional strategic position following Assad regime collapse and Hezbollah degradation.
regime_fall.last_major_event: U.S.-Iran peace talks in Islamabad collapsed after 21 hours on April 12, 2026 with no deal reached, with VP Vance citing Iran's refusal to commit against developing nuclear weapons.
